# Test basic mcptrace-to-otel functionality
go build -o mcptrace-to-otel .

# Create a test MCP trace file
cat > test.mcp << 'EOF'
# mcptrace:v1
mcp-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05"}} # 1234567890.123
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{}}} # 1234567890.124
mcp-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"method":"tools/list","params":{}} # 1234567890.125
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"result":{"tools":[]}} # 1234567890.126
EOF

# Test help output
! ./mcptrace-to-otel -h
stderr 'Usage.*mcptrace-to-otel'

# Test basic conversion
./mcptrace-to-otel test.mcp
stdout 'spans'
stdout 'initialize'
stdout 'tools/list'

# Test output to file
./mcptrace-to-otel -output output.json test.mcp
[ -f output.json ]
grep 'initialize' output.json

# Test with non-existent file
! ./mcptrace-to-otel nonexistent.mcp
stderr 'error.*nonexistent.mcp'

# Clean up
rm -f mcptrace-to-otel test.mcp output.json
